text: Eli Pariser's 2011 concept of the filter bubble describes how algorithmic personalization isolates users inside information environments shaped entirely by their prior clicks. The mechanism is economic: engagement generates data, data trains algorithms, algorithms amplify whatever previously held attention. Outrage and confirmation are extremely efficient at holding attention; therefore, algorithms systematically surface outrage and confirmation. The result is not a propaganda ministry imposing a single view but something more insidious — millions of individually tailored realities, each optimized not for truth or civic health but for the conversion of attention into advertising revenue.
question: According to Pariser's filter bubble concept, why do algorithms systematically surface outrage and confirmation?

options.text: Platforms deliberately want to radicalize users for political influence
These emotions are extremely efficient at holding attention, which generates the data that trains the algorithm
Users explicitly request more emotionally intense content in their preferences
Advertisers pay premiums to appear alongside high-emotion content
